title: Makar Sankranti
intro: Makar Sankranti is one of the most important Hindu festivals, celebrated to mark the Sun’s transition into Makara (Capricorn) and the beginning of Uttarayana, the Sun’s northward journey.
description:
Makar Sankranti is one of the most important Hindu festivals, celebrated to mark the Sun’s transition into Makara (Capricorn) and the beginning of Uttarayana, the Sun’s northward journey. Unlike most Hindu festivals that follow the lunar calendar, Makar Sankranti is based on the solar calendar and is observed every year on 14 January (occasionally on 15 January). The festival is considered highly auspicious for holy bathing, charity, worship of Surya Dev (the Sun God), and spiritual practices.

According to Hindu traditions, the beginning of Uttarayana marks a period of positivity, prosperity, and spiritual growth. It is believed that performing acts of charity, offering prayers to Surya Dev, and taking a holy dip in sacred rivers on this day bring immense merit and divine blessings. The festival is also associated with the harvest season, expressing gratitude to nature, the Sun, and farmers for a successful crop.

Makar Sankranti is celebrated across India under different regional names, including Pongal in Tamil Nadu, Uttarayan in Gujarat, Magh Bihu in Assam, and Khichdi in parts of North India. Devotees offer water to the Sun, donate sesame seeds (til), jaggery, food, and clothing, fly kites, prepare traditional sweets, and celebrate with family and friends. The festival symbolizes gratitude, generosity, new beginnings, and the triumph of light over darkness.

Festival Month

Pausha (January)

Significance / Importance

Makar Sankranti holds immense religious, spiritual, and cultural significance in Hinduism. It marks the beginning of Uttarayana, which is considered an auspicious period for performing religious rituals, charitable acts, and spiritual practices. Devotees worship Surya Dev to seek good health, prosperity, success, and positive energy.

The festival also celebrates the harvest season and encourages gratitude toward nature, the Sun, and the farming community. Donating sesame seeds, jaggery, food, clothing, and other essentials is believed to bring blessings and remove negativity. Makar Sankranti inspires people to embrace generosity, righteousness, hope, and new beginnings.

Puja Vidhi

1. Wake up early and take a holy bath.
2. Offer water (Arghya) to Surya Dev while chanting Surya Mantras.
3. Worship Surya Dev with red flowers, Akshata, sesame seeds, and incense.
4. Recite the Aditya Hridaya Stotra or Surya Mantras.
5. Donate sesame seeds (til), jaggery, food, clothes, blankets, or other essentials.
6. Prepare and offer traditional foods made from sesame seeds and jaggery.
7. Pray for health, prosperity, happiness, and spiritual progress.
8. Celebrate the festival with family by sharing sweets and expressing gratitude.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Makar Sankranti Katha
* Surya Mantra
* Aditya Hridaya Stotra
* Surya Aarti
* Surya Chalisa
* Gayatri Mantra

faq.a1: Makar Sankranti is celebrated every year on 14 January (occasionally on 15 January).
faq.a2: It marks the Sun’s transition into Capricorn (Makara) and the beginning of Uttarayana, symbolizing prosperity, new beginnings, and spiritual growth.
faq.q3: Which deity is worshipped on Makar Sankranti?
faq.a3: Surya Dev (the Sun God) is worshipped on Makar Sankranti.
faq.q4: In which month is Makar Sankranti celebrated?
faq.a4: Makar Sankranti is celebrated in January, during the Hindu month of Pausha.
faq.q5: Why are sesame seeds (til) and jaggery offered on Makar Sankranti?
faq.a5: Til and jaggery symbolize purity, warmth, harmony, and goodwill, and offering or donating them is considered highly auspicious.
faq.q6: What are the main rituals of Makar Sankranti?
faq.a6: Holy bathing, offering Arghya to Surya Dev, charity, Surya Puja, preparing sesame sweets, and flying kites are among the main traditions.
faq.q7: What are the regional names of Makar Sankranti?
faq.a7: The festival is known as Pongal, Uttarayan, Magh Bihu, and Khichdi in different parts of India.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Makar Sankranti?
faq.a8: Makar Sankranti symbolizes gratitude, righteousness, charity, spiritual awakening, positive energy, and the beginning of an auspicious phase marked by Uttarayana.
